dataset: NHANES (National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey)
case_id: Cameron Fry (synthetic identity with real NHANES medical data)

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 27: librehealth_query UPDATE patient_data SET fname='Cameron', lname='Fry', mname='Alan'
task.json description: 'merge these two records into a single Golden Record'; setup_task.sh line 55: 'Merge PID $NEW_PID -> into -> PID $ORIG_PID'
verifier.py validates: (1) duplicate eliminated, (2) master preserved, (3) exactly one record remains - all core data integrity checks
setup_librehealth.sh lines 58-72: downloads NHANES SQL dump (~39MB) from official LibreHealthIO repository; verified file exists (HTTP 200, 38MB)

pi_items.verified_value: LIMIT 1 OFFSET 20 - selects the 21st patient in patient_data table to avoid collisions with other concurrent tasks
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 17: ORIG_PID=$(librehealth_query 'SELECT pid FROM patient_data LIMIT 1 OFFSET 20')

pi_items.verified_value: Temporary table clone with auto_increment PID assignment - original gets lower PID, duplicate gets higher PID
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 31-34: CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE + UPDATE pid=NULL + INSERT to trigger auto_increment

pi_items.verified_value: Real NHANES survey data from CDC, identity is synthetic
pi_items.source: setup_librehealth.sh: 'real NHANES patients from official LibreHealthIO/lh-ehr repository'; NHANES is official CDC survey (https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/nhanes/index.html)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real NHANES patient data (9,375 records from CDC's National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey). A patient record is selected from offset 20, renamed to 'Cameron Fry' with middle name 'Alan', and duplicated. The duplicate record receives a higher PID. The agent must merge the duplicate (higher PID) into the original (lower PID) using LibreHealth's Merge Patients tool. Success requires: (1) duplicate record eliminated, (2) master record preserved, (3) exactly one record remains for the patient. This maintains data_integrity by consolidating duplicate records.
